title: Large gaps between consecutive prime numbers

thm:1.summary: $G(X) \gg R \log X \log_2 X \log_4 X / (\log_3 X)^2$ for any $R$, sufficiently large $X$. Same lower bound on prime gaps as Maynard 1408.5110:thm:1, proved independently and simultaneously.

lem:1.1.summary: Westzynthius-style reduction: $G(P(x) + Y(x) + x) \ge Y(x)$. Reduces 'large gaps' to 'find a long interval coprime to all primes $\le x$'.

thm:2.summary: Quantitative $Y(x) \gg R x \log x \log_3 x / (\log_2 x)^2$. The Jacobsthal-function type bound from which thm:1 follows.

thm:3.summary: First reduction: for $r \ge 13$, with $y = (r/(6\log r)) \cdot x \log x \log_3 x / (\log_2 x)^2$, there exist residue classes $a_s \pmod{s}$ for primes $s \le x$ whose union covers $[1,y]$.

thm:4.summary: Second reduction: choose residue classes only on a 3-part decomposition $\mathcal{S}_1 \cup \mathcal{S}_2 \cup \mathcal{S}_3$ of primes. The $\mathcal{S}_1$ step is easy; $\mathcal{S}_2, \mathcal{S}_3$ carry the analytic difficulty.

thm:5.summary: Third reduction: covering can be restricted to $\mathcal{S}_2 \cup \mathcal{S}_3$ residues hitting all but $o(x/\log x)$ of the prime set $\mathcal{Q} \subset (x/4, y]$.

thm:6.summary: Random covering theorem: with positive probability, random residue classes $a_s$ for $s \in \mathcal{S}_2$ hit the prescribed fraction of $\mathcal{Q}$. The key existence proof — uses entropy / second-moment method on random colorings.

thm:7.summary: Generalized Green-Tao linear-forms-in-primes (Theorem A) under a weakened complexity condition. The analytic engine driving prop:1 and prop:2.

prop:1.summary: Variance bound: $\sum |F(m,n_1)|^2 \Lambda(n_1) = o(yx^2)$ where $F$ counts deviations of $\Lambda$-products from singular series. Quantitative Type II input for thm:6.

prop:2.summary: Companion variance bound for the second residue range (primes near $y$). Together with prop:1 supplies the second-moment estimates the random-covering argument needs.

lem:2.1.summary: Equidistribution count: $\#\{q \in \mathcal{Q}: p \nmid q - ir!p\} \sim \alpha_r y/\log^r x$. Combinatorial input for the covering.

lem:2.2.summary: Disjointness: at most one $i \in [0,r-1]$ with $p \nmid_d q - ir!p$. Ensures the covering is a partition.

lem:2.3.summary: Equidistribution variant for the $d$-divisibility relation. Companion to lem:2.1.

lem:2.4.summary: Existence of dense subsets $\mathcal{P}_0 \subset \mathcal{P}, \mathcal{Q}_0 \subset \mathcal{Q}$ on which the equidistribution counts hold uniformly. Sets up the random model.

lem:3.1.summary: First-moment count: with probability $1-o(1)$, $\#\mathcal{Q}(\vec{\mathbf{a}}) \sim (r/(2\log r)) \cdot x/\log x$. Mean of the random covering.

lem:3.2.summary: Existence of subsets $\mathcal{P}_1 \subset \mathcal{P}_0, \mathcal{Q}_1 \subset \mathcal{Q}_0$ with prescribed cardinalities and uniform equidistribution. Random refinement of lem:2.4.

lem:4.1.summary: Singular-series factorization $\gamma_i \sim \gamma^i$ uniformly for $i \le 2r$. Independence-style estimate.

lem:4.2.summary: Concentration of degree-$r$ covering counts around their mean $\gamma^r \alpha_r y/\log^r x$. Drives the second-moment argument.

edges.note: First reduction sets up the covering target.
Third reduction restricts the covered set further to $\mathcal{Q}$.
Generalized Green-Tao Theorem A is the analytic engine.
Companion variance bound for the second range.
Variance computed via Theorem A linear-forms-in-primes.
First moment uses the equidistribution count.

main_result_and_reduction.description: Westzynthius reduces gaps to $Y(x)$. The chain thm:2 $\to$ thm:3 $\to$ thm:4 $\to$ thm:5 reduces 'find $Y(x)$' to 'cover residues with primes in $\mathcal{S}_2 \cup \mathcal{S}_3$'.

random_covering.description: thm:6 — random choice of residue classes succeeds with positive probability. The probabilistic core, distinguishing FGKT from Maynard's deterministic sieve.

linear_forms_in_primes.description: thm:7 (generalized Green-Tao Theorem A) supplies the analytic input. Variance estimates prop:1, prop:2 are second-moment computations using thm:7.

combinatorial_input.description: lem:2.1-2.4: equidistribution counts and disjointness; lem:3.1-3.2: probabilistic existence; lem:4.1-4.2: singular-series asymptotics.

GreenTao:linear.title: Linear equations in primes
GreenTao:linear.role: foundational — Theorem A invoked as thm:7

GreenTaoZiegler:gowers.title: An inverse theorem for the Gowers $U^{s+1}$ norm
GreenTaoZiegler:gowers.role: foundational — Gowers norm inverse theorem; needed for Linear Forms

Maynard:largegaps.title: Large gaps between primes
Maynard:largegaps.role: parallel — independent simultaneous proof of the same theorem via different machinery

ErdosRankin:1938.title: On the difference of consecutive primes

Westzynthius:1931.title: Über die Verteilung der Zahlen, die zu den n ersten Primzahlen teilerfremd sind
